content.problemStatement:
You are given an array A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of length NNN. Each distinct element appears twice except for one. Find that single one.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the single one.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the element that appears exactly once.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: array [5] → single element is 5.
Test case 2: array [2,5,2,10,10] → 5 appears once.
Test case 3: array [1,1,10,10,15] → 15 appears once.
Test case 4: array [6,8,10,6,8] → 10 appears once.
Only 42 does not have a duplicate.
First test case single element is 1000000000. Second test case array has pairs (1,1) and (2,2); 3 is alone.

solution.approach: All numbers except one appear exactly twice. XOR of a number with itself is 0 and XOR with 0 yields the number itself. Therefore, XORing all elements of the array leaves only the single element. Iterate through the array, maintain a variable ans initialized to 0, and ans ^= Ai for each i. After processing the whole test case, ans is the required number.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total N) – each element is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        ans = 0
        for _ in range(N):
            ans ^= int(next(it))
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
